name: minara
tagline: ClawHub (109★): Crypto trading & wallet, and AI market analysis via Minara CLI. Swap, perps, transfer, deposit (credit card/crypto), withdr
verdict: Security and quality check out, but there's an internal contradiction: the file claims it won't run bash/CLI commands, yet its own steps invoke bash and even an rm -f delete — worth verifying what gets removed. The description is also a ~780-character wall of text, and installs float on an unpinned @latest.

highlights.summary: Directly contradicts the rest of the file, which repeatedly instructs the agent to run bash/CLI commands, e.g. line 20 `bash {baseDir}/scripts/version-check.sh` and line 39 `rm -f
The description field itself is a ~780-character paragraph-length wall enumerating features and trigger categories, matching the rubric's own fail-pattern description of oversized
Global install pinned to floating `@latest` rather than an exact/ranged version (also present in frontmatter metadata.install, line 6); source is the standard npm registry and the
